text:
Once upon a time, in a small village nestled between rolling hills and whispering woods, there grew an oak tree so elderly it had seen many empires rise and fall. Its bark was tough as leather, its branches heavy with centuries-old branches laden with acorns. Every autumn, children would come looking for the magic oak leaf that would grant them three wishes.

One particular autumn, a young girl named Marina wandered through the forest hoping to find this legendary leaf. As she traveled, her dog, a fluffy white mutt named Bartholombius, bounded alongside her, his tail wagging like a thousand tiny flags.

After hours of searching, Marina reached the old oak and knelt beside its ancient trunk. Bartholombius barked joyfully, sensing excitement.

“Just leave me alone, Bartholombius,” Marina sighed, brushing grains of fallen leaves from her clothes.
